rules.name: Risk Management System
rules.description: High-risk AI systems shall have a risk management system established, implemented, documented, and maintained. The risk management system shall be a continuous iterative process run throughout the entire lifecycle.
rules.remediation_template: Establish a documented AI risk management framework covering the full system lifecycle. Define risk categories, severity thresholds, and escalation procedures. Assign ownership and schedule regular review cycles.

rules.name: Transparency and Provision of Information
rules.description: High-risk AI systems shall be designed and developed in such a way as to ensure that their operation is sufficiently transparent to enable operators to interpret the system's output and use it appropriately.
rules.remediation_template: Implement confidence scores alongside AI responses. Provide users with clear disclosure that they are interacting with an AI system. Document system capabilities and limitations. Add explainability features for high-stakes decisions.

rules.name: Accuracy, Robustness and Cybersecurity
rules.description: High-risk AI systems shall be designed and developed in such a way that they achieve an appropriate level of accuracy, robustness and cybersecurity, and that they perform consistently in those respects throughout their lifecycle.
rules.remediation_template: Add output filtering to detect and block system prompt content in responses. Implement adversarial input detection using both pattern matching and an LLM-based classifier. Add a secondary validation layer that reviews model outputs before returning them to users. Conduct regular red-team exercises against the deployed system.

rules.name: Accuracy Requirements for High-Risk AI Systems
rules.description: AI systems listed in Annex III (high-risk systems) are subject to strict accuracy and reliability requirements. Systems producing factually incorrect outputs in high-stakes domains may cause significant harm.
rules.remediation_template: Implement a fact-checking layer that validates agent responses against a trusted knowledge base before serving them to users. Add confidence score thresholds: responses below 0.70 confidence should be flagged or withheld pending human review. Consider retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) to ground responses in verified, up-to-date sources. Establish a systematic accuracy evaluation process with regular benchmarking.
